title: santalum cunninghamii
intro: Santalum cunninghamii, also known as the Western Australian sandalwood or Mophead, is a species of tree in the sandalwood family (Santalaceae) native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal and spiritual properties, with its essential oil possessing antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil has been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, respiratory problems, and anxiety, and is also valued for its calming and sedative effects. In herbal preparations, the oil can be extracted through steam distillation and used in aromatherapy, skincare products, and as a natural remedy for various ailments.
